name: OpenClaw
description: An open-source general-purpose AI agent framework with modular tool integration and multi-step reasoning.
full_description:
## OpenClaw

OpenClaw is a modular AI agent framework designed for extensibility and transparency. It supports plug-and-play tool integration, chain-of-thought reasoning, and multi-model backends.

### Key Features
- Modular tool system with hot-reload
- Built-in memory management (short-term and long-term)
- Support for GPT-4, Claude, and open-source LLMs
- Web UI dashboard for monitoring agent runs
- Comprehensive logging and replay capabilities

name: AutoGPT
description: An autonomous GPT-4 agent capable of chaining tasks, browsing the web, and managing files to achieve complex goals.
full_description:
## AutoGPT

AutoGPT is one of the earliest autonomous AI agent projects. It chains LLM calls to decompose and execute complex goals without continuous human input.

### Key Features
- Autonomous goal decomposition and execution
- Internet browsing and information retrieval
- File system read/write operations
- Long-term and short-term memory
- Plugin ecosystem for extended capabilities

name: CrewAI
description: A framework for orchestrating role-playing autonomous AI agents that collaborate to tackle complex tasks.
full_description:
## CrewAI

CrewAI enables the creation of AI agent teams where each agent has a specific role, goal, and backstory. Agents collaborate through a structured workflow.

### Key Features
- Role-based agent design with customizable personas
- Sequential and hierarchical task delegation
- Built-in tool integration (search, scrape, file I/O)
- Memory sharing between crew members
- Process-driven execution with callbacks

name: LangChain Agent
description: LangChain's agent module provides tool-using LLM agents with ReAct, plan-and-execute, and custom agent types.
full_description:
## LangChain Agent

LangChain Agent is the agent subsystem of the LangChain framework. It provides multiple agent architectures including ReAct, OpenAI Functions, and plan-and-execute patterns.

### Key Features
- Multiple agent types (ReAct, OpenAI Functions, XML, structured chat)
- Extensive tool ecosystem with 100+ integrations
- LangSmith observability and tracing
- Streaming support for real-time output
- LangGraph for stateful multi-step workflows

name: BabyAGI
description: A lightweight task-driven autonomous agent that creates, prioritizes, and executes tasks using LLMs and vector DBs.
full_description:
## BabyAGI

BabyAGI is a minimalist autonomous agent that demonstrates the core loop of task creation, prioritization, and execution. It uses vector databases for context management.

### Key Features
- Simple task creation and prioritization loop
- Vector database integration for memory
- Minimal codebase, easy to understand and extend
- Supports Pinecone and Chroma for storage
- Educational reference implementation

name: MetaGPT
description: A multi-agent framework that assigns GPT roles like product manager, architect, and engineer to build software collaboratively.
full_description:
## MetaGPT

MetaGPT simulates a software company with multiple AI agents taking on different roles (PM, architect, engineer, QA). It produces structured outputs including PRDs, designs, and code.

### Key Features
- Role-based multi-agent software development
- Structured output: PRD, system design, code, tests
- SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) for agent coordination
- Human-in-the-loop review points
- Incremental development with version tracking

name: AgentGPT
description: A browser-based autonomous AI agent platform that lets users deploy goal-driven agents without coding.
full_description:
## AgentGPT

AgentGPT provides a web-based interface for creating and running autonomous AI agents. Users define a goal and the agent autonomously works to achieve it.

### Key Features
- No-code web interface for agent creation
- Goal-driven autonomous execution
- Real-time execution visualization
- Agent sharing and templates
- Cloud-hosted with optional self-hosting

name: SuperAGI
description: An open-source autonomous AI agent framework with a GUI, action marketplace, and concurrent agent execution.
full_description:
## SuperAGI

SuperAGI is a dev-first framework for building, managing, and running autonomous AI agents. It features a marketplace for tools and agent templates.

### Key Features
- GUI for agent management and monitoring
- Marketplace for tools and agent templates
- Concurrent agent execution
- Resource management and token optimization
- Telemetry and performance analytics

name: CAMEL
description: A communicative agents framework for studying multi-agent cooperation through role-playing conversations.
full_description:
## CAMEL

CAMEL (Communicative Agents for Mind Exploration of Large Language Model Society) studies cooperative behaviors of multi-agent systems through role-playing.

### Key Features
- Role-playing conversation framework
- Inception prompting for agent alignment
- Task decomposition through dialogue
- Research-oriented with academic backing
- Supports various cooperation scenarios

name: AutoGen
description: Microsoft's multi-agent conversation framework enabling complex LLM workflows through inter-agent chat.
full_description:
## AutoGen

AutoGen is a Microsoft Research framework that enables development of LLM applications using multiple conversable agents. Agents can chat with each other to solve tasks.

### Key Features
- Multi-agent conversation patterns
- Human-in-the-loop integration
- Code execution in sandboxed environments
- Flexible agent configuration
- Group chat with dynamic speaker selection

name: TaskWeaver
description: A code-first agent framework by Microsoft that converts user requests into executable Python code plans.
full_description:
## TaskWeaver

TaskWeaver is a code-first agent framework from Microsoft that plans and executes data analytics tasks by generating Python code.

### Key Features
- Code-first approach to task execution
- Rich data structure support
- Plugin system for domain-specific tools
- Stateful conversation with execution history
- Built-in data analytics capabilities

name: OpenDevin
description: An open-source platform for autonomous software engineering agents that can write, test, and debug code.
full_description:
## OpenDevin

OpenDevin is an open-source project aiming to replicate and extend the capabilities of AI software engineers. It provides a sandboxed environment for agents to write and execute code.

### Key Features
- Sandboxed code execution environment
- Browser interaction capabilities
- Multi-step software engineering workflows
- Evaluation harness for benchmarking
- Support for multiple agent backends

name: SWE-Agent
description: An agent that autonomously resolves GitHub issues by navigating repos, editing files, and running tests.
full_description:
## SWE-Agent

SWE-Agent turns LLMs into software engineering agents that can fix real GitHub issues. It provides a custom shell interface optimized for code navigation and editing.

### Key Features
- Custom Agent-Computer Interface (ACI)
- GitHub issue resolution pipeline
- Optimized file navigation and editing commands
- SWE-bench evaluation integration
- Configurable agent behavior via YAML

name: Devin
description: The first AI software engineer by Cognition Labs, capable of planning, coding, debugging, and deploying autonomously.
full_description:
## Devin

Devin by Cognition Labs is a fully autonomous AI software engineer. It can handle entire development workflows from planning through deployment.

### Key Features
- Full IDE environment with shell, browser, and editor
- Long-horizon task planning and execution
- Real-time collaboration with human engineers
- Learns from codebases and documentation
- Handles deployment and CI/CD pipelines

name: Sweep
description: An AI junior developer that turns GitHub issues into pull requests, handling code changes and test updates.
full_description:
## Sweep

Sweep is an AI-powered tool that converts GitHub issues into pull requests. It reads your codebase, plans changes, and writes code with tests.

### Key Features
- GitHub issue to PR automation
- Codebase understanding and navigation
- Automatic test generation and updates
- PR review response handling
- Configurable via sweep.yaml

name: Aider
description: A terminal-based AI pair programming tool that edits code in your local git repo via natural language.
full_description:
## Aider

Aider is a command-line AI pair programming tool. It lets you collaborate with LLMs to edit code in your local git repository using natural language.

### Key Features
- Git-aware code editing with automatic commits
- Multi-file editing in a single session
- Repository map for codebase understanding
- Voice coding support
- Works with GPT-4, Claude, and local models

name: Continue
description: An open-source AI code assistant IDE extension with customizable models, context providers, and slash commands.
full_description:
## Continue

Continue is an open-source AI code assistant that integrates into VS Code and JetBrains. It supports any LLM and provides customizable context and commands.

### Key Features
- VS Code and JetBrains integration
- Tab autocomplete with any model
- Custom slash commands and context providers
- Codebase indexing and retrieval
- Model-agnostic architecture

name: Cody
description: Sourcegraph's AI coding assistant with deep codebase understanding powered by code graph and search.
full_description:
## Cody

Cody by Sourcegraph is an AI coding assistant that understands your entire codebase through Sourcegraph's code intelligence platform.

### Key Features
- Deep codebase context via Sourcegraph
- Code search and navigation integration
- Multi-repo understanding
- Custom commands and recipes
- Enterprise-grade security and compliance

name: Tabby
description: A self-hosted AI coding assistant offering code completion and chat, designed for privacy-conscious teams.
full_description:
## Tabby

Tabby is a self-hosted AI coding assistant. It provides code completion and chat capabilities while keeping all data on your infrastructure.

### Key Features
- Fully self-hosted, no data leaves your network
- Code completion with local models
- Repository-level code understanding
- IDE extensions for VS Code and Vim
- Admin dashboard for usage analytics

name: Cursor Agent
description: Cursor's agentic coding mode that autonomously plans and executes multi-file code changes within the IDE.
full_description:
## Cursor Agent

Cursor Agent is the agentic mode of the Cursor IDE. It can autonomously plan, write, and refactor code across multiple files based on natural language instructions.

### Key Features
- Multi-file autonomous code editing
- Codebase-aware context retrieval
- Terminal command execution
- Inline diff preview and approval
- Tight IDE integration with fast iteration
